headline: 'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
description: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'

headline: Iran strikes Gulf neighbors after new US attacks
'I would do a line before service, hidden in the bathroom': How cocaine is flooding the restaurant industry
France powers down several nuclear reactors due to extreme heat
description: Three nuclear reactors were temporarily shut down and eight others were operating at reduced power, France's main energy provider said.

headline: Lindsey Graham, Republican hawk who became staunch Trump loyalist, has died
description: The senator from South Carolina, who was seeking re-election in November, died suddenly at the age of 71. He embodied, perhaps better than anyone else, the Republican Party's surrender to Trumpism.

headline: Martine Barrat, a photographer without fear or limits
description: At 93, the artist has lost none of her vitality or curiosity. She lives at the legendary Hotel Chelsea in New York, where she remains one of the last year-round artist residents. Today, her work is the subject of a retrospective at the Rencontres d'Arles photography festival.

headline: Macron's tribute to Alfred Dreyfus: 'Dreyfusism reminds us that antisemitism is the enemy of the Republic'
description: French President Emmanuel Macron spoke at the first national day commemorating the recognition of Alfred Dreyfus's innocence, 120 years after the army captain wrongfully convicted of treason was exonerated.

headline: Spanish PM Sanchez condemns predecessor's racist remarks on French football team
Zelensky announces Ukrainian government reshuffle, including PM change
The rush to air-conditioned spaces: 'Full house at the cinema for 2 hours and 40 minutes of AC'
Tour de France: Van der Poel wins shortened ninth stage
Pauline Vilain-Carlotti, geographer: 'The intensity of wildfires, which seems unprecedented, could become the new norm'
Qatar's former ruler Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies at 74
Why heatwaves disrupt our sleep – and how we can protect it
Tiago Rodrigues, director of the Avignon Festival: 'Thanks to Europe, thanks to democracy, how lucky I have been!'
Before Emile Zola, this forgotten writer was the first to defend Alfred Dreyfus
How live screenings of Paris Fashion Week are making fashion more accessible
'No region is safe from climate change: Brittany will not be a climate refuge'
Kyiv spends sleepless nights underground as Russia intensifies attacks
'You're expected to perform, but no one wants to know how you do it': Cocaine, the toxic fuel of young professionals
description: Cocaine at work (1/3). Among young professionals, the illegal and highly addictive drug is used in a relentless pursuit of performance, often shrouded in taboo and without questioning of working conditions.

headline: How Ukraine is turning Crimea, the Russians' Riviera, into a battlefield
description: The annexed Ukrainian peninsula, which Vladimir Putin sees as his crowning jewel, has descended into chaos as Kyiv ramps up attacks on the territory. Shocked tourists and residents express their distress as shortages worsen.

headline: Silicon Valley's next generation feels the AI blues
description: In California, the rise of artificial intelligence no longer generates the unanimous enthusiasm it once did. Anxiety is mounting, especially among students and engineers, as jobs have been cut and democratic risks have grown more threatening.

headline: 'Imperial obsession and reckless escalation: Vladimir Putin has never appeared so trapped by his fantasies'
'The UAE does not want its strategic partnership with Israel to depend on Benjamin Netanyahu'
'The latest chapter in Le Pen's legal saga illustrates the intractable challenge of judging politicians'
